title: AI Integration
description: Call Claude from a durable resource handler, sliced from the page-writer example.
client_intro: The page-writer wraps the Anthropic SDK behind a small interface. <0>HtmlWriterClient</0> declares the one method the app uses, and <1>createHtmlWriterClient</1> builds the real client from the environment:
client_notice: This interface is the dependency-injection seam. Production code passes nothing and gets the real SDK, while tests hand <0>writeHtml</0> a scripted fake — no network, no API key.
request_intro: <0>writeHtml</0> sends the user's prompt with a fixed system prompt and defaults its <1>client</1> parameter to the real SDK:
request_notice: The system prompt pins the output contract — one standalone HTML document, no markdown fences — so the rest of the pipeline can treat the response as a deployable artifact instead of chat.
extract_intro: Responses arrive as content blocks. <0>extractHtmlDocument</0> pulls the first text block and fails loudly when Claude returns nothing usable:
generate_intro: The Page component turns one prompt into one durable resource. Its handler restores from the ledger first and only calls <0>writeHtml</0> when the page has never been generated — that check is what makes it idempotent:
generate_notice: <0>setOutputs(prev => …)</0> receives the last persisted value, even across restarts. If the previous run already reached <1>ready</1>, the handler keeps that state and returns without spending another API call.
tip_persistence: AI outputs persist like any other <0>useAsyncOutput</0> result. Restarting the app re-runs every handler, but restored pages skip generation — only genuinely new prompts reach the API.
see_walkthrough: See the full prompt-to-page pipeline in the <0>page-writer walkthrough</0>.
